name: Ohio Medical Trnsprtn Non
recent_news: No public news found; very small NEMT operator in Columbus area. Site/domain not clearly resolvable; multiple similar-named NEMT firms operate in Franklin County.
fit_notes: Not a fit. Micro-scale NEMT (~6 employees, ~$570K rev) with no evidence of school-bus / SPED route contracts. Below Summit tuck-in threshold.

name: Eagle Cabulance LLC
districts_or_customers: Seattle-area special education students (per fleet listing as STVs)
recent_news: BBB-listed Seattle cabulance operator; vehicles registered as Student Transportation Vehicles per WA fleet records. No M&A activity. Active 2026.
fit_notes: Marginal fit. Independent owner-operator with some SPED-to-school work in greater Seattle, but micro-scale (~7 employees, ~$560K). Possible bolt-in to a larger WA SPED platform, not a standalone Summit target.

name: Cmi Mobility
districts_or_customers: Retail individuals with mobility needs; GSA/SAM patient-mobility-device contract
recent_news: Operates as wheelchair-accessible vehicle conversion/dealer ('34 years providing vehicles to people with special needs'). Listed under SAM.gov code 65IIF (Patient Mobility Devices). Not a transportation operator.
fit_notes: Not a fit. Mobility vehicle CONVERSION/DEALER, not a student-transport operator. Misclassified in source data. Exclude.

name: GC Logistics LLC
districts_or_customers: Federal, state, municipal, and private clients (govtribe-listed federal vendor)
recent_news: Active federal contractor since 2017 in Ridgeland, MS; D&B lists 'School and Employee Bus Transportation' as a NAICS but website/marketing emphasizes grounds maintenance, sanitation, and supply chain - no evidence of operating school-bus routes.
fit_notes: Not a fit. Diversified facilities/O&M/logistics firm, not a school-bus contractor. NAICS code likely misleading. Exclude.

name: Speedy Wheelchair Trnsprtn
recent_news: No public news; Charlotte CMS routes special-needs school transport in-house or via CMS Transportation Office, with private wheelchair vendors used for medical trips. No evidence Speedy holds CMS SPED contracts.
fit_notes: Not a fit. Micro wheelchair/NEMT operator (~7 employees, ~$505K rev) with no confirmed school-district contracts. Below Summit tuck-in threshold.
